reasoning: The average daily temperature trend in Toulouse falls from mid-Autumn (Nov) into Winter (Dec-Feb). Historically, an early November day is expected to be warmer than a day in December or February, meaning the basic physical probability of later dates exceeding the Nov 9 temperature is well below 50% (often around 25-35%). To comply with the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I have scaled these baseline probabilities upwards over time, maintaining the physical reality (all < 0.5) while ensuring the strict monotonic convergence toward 0.5 as requested.
